article_items.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
article_items.description: Subquadratic has now shared more details about its new model. But some are still skeptical.
article_items.excerpt: Subquadratic has now shared more details about its new model. But some are still skeptical.
article_items.summary: The company also claims that SubQ is able to process up to 12 times as much text at once than most other models, allowing it to carry out a range of data-heavy tasks, such as analyzing hundreds of documents or entire code bases. key_points: On LiveCodeBench, a test that looks at how well models perform on competitive coding problems taken from real contests, SubQ scored 89.7%, putting it in the same ballpark as other top coding models .

One nagging issue is that Subquadratic reused the weights ( values set within a model during training that determine how it will behave ) from a version of the Chinese open-source model Qwen to bootstrap SubQ, rather.

The company also claims that SubQ is able to process up to 12 times as much text at once than most other models, allowing it to carry out a range of data-heavy tasks, such as analyzing hundreds of documents or entire.

Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models Michelle Kim China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip - here’s what’s next.

In a straight-up speed test, which sets a baseline for how fast a model can operate in theory rather than assess what a model can actually do, Appen found that SubQ was 56 times faster than models using FlashAttention.
According to Dangel, it costs $2600 to run Anthropic's LLM Opus 4.6 through RULER 128, a test developed by Nvidia to assess a model's ability to retrieve information from large data sets.
Subquadratic is offering SubQ as a model tailored to coding and to searching very large data sets.
As the length of the text increases, the number of computations skyrockets.
Dan McAteer, an artificial intelligence engineer, captured the overall response on X : “SubQ is either the biggest breakthrough since the Transformer ...
It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade.
The model has a context window (roughly akin to a working memory) up to 12 million tokens long.
What’s more, Subquadratic says, SubQ does this while more or less matching the performance of the best models put out by Google DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic on key tasks like coding.
